source: Deep Neural Networks for YouTube Recommendations

concepts.finding: Two-stage retrieval + ranking funnel. Candidate generation: extreme multiclass classification (millions of video classes) with sampled softmax (importance-weighted negative sampling). Learned user embeddings as function of watch history, search tokens, demographics. At serving: nearest neighbor search in embedding space. Ranking: separate DNN predicting expected watch time (not CTR) via weighted logistic regression. 'Example Age' feature captures freshness (time since upload).
concepts.implication: Two-stage architecture can apply: (1) Stage 1: keyword -> intent candidate generation via softmax over intent classes. (2) Stage 2: rank intents by relevance to keyword. Freshness feature ('Example Age') is critical if keywords have temporal trends.
concepts.open_question: Should keyword-intent matching be a single-stage scoring or two-stage (candidate generation + ranking) as in YouTube?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: A two-stage keyword-intent model (generation with sampled softmax + ranking with DNN) scales better to 1000+ intents than a single flat classifier.
